sm_more_helpful_text: Is social media a good thing? Due to social media, there has been a sharp decline in human interaction and social skills. In addition, when hiring employees or accepting students into colleges, many institutions are checking social media background, which can be harmful. Finally, because of increased online presence, identity theft has become a bigger threat than ever before. Despite the advantages the comes from, Facebook, Snapchat, and others have the potential to ruin peopleâ<U+0080><U+0099>s lives. / The art of conversation is fading away. Rather than have create banter with a friend, people are more likely to Snapchat each other. People are too focused at their devices rather than speaking to one another. Looking at another person in the eye and having a productive conversation has become a lost art. This lack of communications can be detrimental in many ways. / Applying for a job is not what it was ten years ago. Now potential employers will check an applicantâ<U+0080><U+0099>s online footprint for any signs of bad behavior in his or her personal life. Making a childish mistake on social media may destroy a personâ<U+0080><U+0099>s chances of being accepted into college or getting a job. A mistake made at a young age could follow a person for his or her entire life. Posting personal information on social media can open a person up to a variety of dangerous consequences.  / Now with advanced technology people can hack your social media account and steal your identity.  From there people can access an innocent personâ<U+0080><U+0099>s email and credit card information and destroy their personal and social life. This can lead people to becoming broke and losing their job and friends.  Despite their best efforts to keep thieves at bay, there are always people looking to take advantage of people who have made their identities online. The bigger a personâ<U+0080><U+0099>s online presence, the more susceptible he or she is to danger. / Social media sites have had a devastating impact on the lives of many people, socially, financially, and personally.  The  heroic woman who stood up for her rights, Malala Yousafzai, once said, â<U+0080><U+009C>What is interesting is the power and the impact of social mediaâ<U+0080>¦ So we must try to use it in a good way.â<U+0080>ť Cyber bullying is just one example of the dangers people face if they donâ<U+0080><U+0099>t use social media responsibly. Just as in facet of life, if people donâ<U+0080><U+0099>t use the internet in the right way, they will become victims of their own reckless actions. The negative effects of social media greatly outnumber the positive ones, and people would be wise to shut down their devices and actually have a conversation with the people they are with. /  /  /  /  /  /  /   /

adults_worry_text: They should be worried about cyber-bullying and ignorance, especially with tensions rising because of the presidential election.
sm_more_helpful_text: There are both positive and negative attributes to social media in assisting my personal relationships. At times, it may help because it is easier to share information that can be discussed with my friends. For example, if I found a music video I really liked p, I can share it with them and we can have a conversation about it. It also aids me with keeping in touch with old friends who may moved away because of college. However, it can also be detrimental to some relationships I have seen because people will post nasty things about other people or write things that may offend or be aimed towards a specific person. It can hurt relationships in this way because people are not face-to-face, therefore making it easier to write about subjects that may be difficult to say in person .
